etymology.description: Depicts uncertain plant roots or an animal, possibly related to 豕(豕) (shǐ, "pig")
etymology.interpretation: 亥 (hài) is traditionally understood as a pictographic depiction with unclear specifics. Some scholars suggest it represents the roots of a plant underground, conceptually linked to the original form of 荄(荄) (gāi, "roots"). Alternatively, it might illustrate an animal shape, potentially related to 豕(豕) (shǐ, "pig"), contributing to its association with the twelfth earthly branch in the Chinese zodiac, signifying the pig.

examples.chinese: 这个年份是猪年，在十二生肖里对应的是亥(這個年份是豬年，在十二生肖里對應的是亥)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège niánfèn shì zhū nián, zài shí'èr shēngxiào lǐ duìyìng de shì hài.
examples.english: This year is the Year of the Pig, corresponding to Hai in the Chinese zodiac.

examples.chinese: 亥时是一天中的第十个时辰，对于夜猫子来说是开始活跃的时候(亥時是一天中的第十個時辰，對於夜貓子來說是開始活躍的時候)。
examples.pinyin: Hài shí shì yītiān zhōng de dì shí gè shíchén, duìyú yèmāozi lái shuō shì kāishǐ huóyuè de shíhòu.
examples.english: The Hai hour is the tenth hour of the day, when night owls begin to be active.

examples.chinese: 据说在古代，亥时正是盗贼活动最猖獗的时候(據說在古代，亥時正是盜賊活動最猖獗的時候)。
examples.pinyin: Jùshuō zài gǔdài, hài shí zhèng shì dàozéi huódòng zuì chāngjué de shíhòu.
examples.english: It's said that in ancient times, the Hai hour was when thieves were most rampant.
